channel.title: Project on My blog
channel.description: Recent content in Project on My blog

item.title: Adelaide CS Community Project
item.description:
In Java, the programmer allocates memory by creating a new object. There is no way to de-allocate that memory. Periodically the Garbage Collector sweeps through the memory allocated to the program, and determines which objects it can safely destroy, therefore releasing the memory.

When I first knew about this feature, I felt it was quite appealing to me, especially for a C++ programmer, because I do not need to worry about the memory leak anymore; therefore I started learning Java in April 2022. On December 22, 2022, I started my Java full-stack project. This post will keep track of my progress of developing this project and my notes of learning.
